Comment: beautiful puzzle... the combinatorics might be little hard... but there is a general formula on wikipedia that allows to compute the calculation in few lines of code... also the tag inclusion-exclusion principle is good its important to solve this puzzle in a medium difficult
ooh... i tried to solve it with counting theory.... but in this puzzle there are intersection of the different groups... no idea how to deal with that.... not good approach i think

Goal
* Please rewrite as follows:

You have a {{60}}-card <<Magic: The Gathering>> deck. Each card is represented by a 3-character string of the form [[T]][[C]][[P]], where:

[[T]] indicates the <<type>> of the card:
• {{C}} = Creature
• {{I}} = Instant
• {{S}} = Sorcery
• {{E}} = Enchantment
• {{L}} = Land

[[C]] represents the <<mana cost>> of the card. It is an integer from {{0}} to {{9}}.

[[P]] indicates the <<color>> of the card:
• {{W}} = White
• {{U}} = Blue
• {{B}} = Black
• {{R}} = Red
• {{G}} = Green

For example:
• {{C1R}} = a {{Red Creature}} card that costs {{1}} mana
• {{L0G}} = a {{Green Land}} card (cost {{0}})
• {{I3B}} = a {{Black Instant}} card costing {{3}} mana

You will draw a {{7}}-card opening hand from the deck, chosen <<randomly without replacement>>. You must compute the probability that your hand matches all of a given set of patterns, i.e. your 7-card hand contains at least one card matching each pattern.

Each pattern is a 3-character string where each character may be a specific value or {{x}}, which matches any value in that position.

For example:
• {{C1x}} = a {{Creature}} card of cost {{1}} and {{any color}}
• {{LxB}} = a {{Land}} card that is {{Black}} and of {{any cost}}
• {{Ixx}} = any {{Instant}} card

== Example ==

Input:
```
7
C1R 10
C2B 5
C3G 8
I2U 12
S4W 5
L0G 15
L0B 5
2
C1x
LxB
```

Output:
0.3334

== Explanation ==

There are {{10}} cards of category {{C1R}} in the deck. {{C1x}} matches all those 10 cards.

There are {{5}} cards of category {{L0B}} in the deck. {{LxB}} matches all those 5 cards.

The probability that a 7-card hand includes at least one card from each of those patterns is approximately 0.3334.

Input
* Please change "{{C}}" to "<<Line 1>>: An integer [[C]]".

* Please change "Next C lines" to "<<Next [[C]] lines>>".

* Please change "{{Category_i}} {{Count_i}}" to "[[Category]] [[Count]]".

* Please add "separated by a space" after "that category".

* Please change "{{Q}}" to "<<Next line>>: An integer [[Q]]".

* Please change "constraints (patterns)" to "patterns".

* Please change "Next Q lines" to "<<Next [[Q]] lines>>".

* Please change "{{Pattern_j}}" to "[[Pattern]]".

* Please change "each a 3-character string using the wildcard x for any cost or color" to "a 3-character string representing a pattern to match, and may contain any number of {{x}}s as wildcards".

Output
* Please change "satisfies all constraints" to "includes at least one card matching each of the given patterns".

Constraints
* Please format "C" and "Q" as [[variables]].

* Please change "{{Count_i}}" to [[Count]].

* Please change the upper limit of [[Q]] to 8.

Tags
* I don't see any "Dynamic programming" or "Bitmasking" in your solution code. Are they relevant to this puzzle?

Stub generator
* Please add a write statement.

[reached the character limit…]

[…continued]

Cases
* 100% (test / validator): Please add a new validator (pattern = "xxx") to pair up with the original test, and add a new test (pattern contains 1 x only and output = 1.0000) to pair up with the original validator.

* Overlap (test): Please delete the trailing newline in the input.

* Overlap (validator): Please delete the trailing newline in the output.

* 3 Patterns (test): The total number of cards isn't 60.

* Please change the second "C1x" to another pattern which doesn't repeat any of the other patterns, while keeping a non-zero output:
   • 6 patterns (validator)
   • 7 patterns (validator)
   • 8 Patterns (test)

* 8 Patterns (validator): Please change the patterns so that they're still non-repeating and the output becomes greater than 0.

* 8 Patterns Impossible (test): Please change "X9x" to "x9x".

* Starting from the "5 Patterns" (test), almost all of the given patterns are in the format of "TCx". Please vary the cases so that many more patterns (TCP, TxP, xCP, Txx, xCx, xxP, xxx) appear.

Thanks.

Yes, what you need here is the inclusion–exclusion principle.

Basically, instead of trying to count “good” hands directly, you:

Count all the hands that fail pattern A, then all that fail pattern B, etc.
Subtract the hands that fail both A and B (because you’ve double-counted them).
Add back the hands that fail A, B and C (since you just subtracted those twice),
And so on, alternating subtraction and addition for every combo of patterns.
Thanks for the approval :)
There is 60 cards in a deck not in total

oh ok
Thank you so much for this review, it helped a lot. It’s my first time trying to create this kind of puzzle, so let me know if I missed anything.

No problem! Most of the issues have been fixed. Below is the updated list of points to follow up:

Constraints
* Please change "{{Count_i}}" to [[Count]].

Cases
* 8 Patterns (validator): Please change "C1x" to "x1R".

* Can you modify "8 Patterns Impossible" (test and validator) to be less trivial by ensuring the output remains 0.0000, but for a reason other than simply having a pattern unavailable in the deck (already covered in the "0%" cases)?

For example, each of the 8 patterns is individually satisfiable by distinct cards in the deck, but because the patterns don't overlap and the opening hand is limited to 7 cards, it's impossible to satisfy all 8 patterns simultaneously.

* Please also ensure a good spread of the mana cost in the cases (currently mostly concentrated in 0 to 4).

Thanks.
Thanks again. I’ve made the modifications, you were right, that was the original objective of the “8 Patterns Impossible” cases, and I lost it while making other changes. 😄
